title: Stocks Steady as Oil Extends Gain to $85
Trump Reinstates Iran Blockade, Plans Hormuz Ship Fee
summary: President Donald Trump reinstated the US blockade of Iranian ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz and demanded a 20% reimbursement for all other cargo shipped via the waterway. Bloomberg's Stuart Livingstone-Wallace and Marisks CEO discuss the implications. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Oil Advances Above $85 on Hormuz Tensions
summary: Brent oil rose above $85 a barrel for the first time in a month, as US President Donald Trump reimposed a blockade on Iranian ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz and demanded payment for all other cargo. Bloomberg's Rong Wei Neo reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Indonesia Says S&P Affirmation to Help Stabilize Bond Market
summary: Indonesia expects S&P Global Ratings’ vote of confidence in its sovereign credit profile to help stabilize the domestic bond market after a selloff in June, according to a top finance ministry official.

title: Brent Tops $85, Rate-Hike Bets Rise Before US CPI: Markets Wrap
summary: Oil advanced for a second day as the standoff between the US and Iran intensified, reigniting inflation concerns and boosting bets on an interest-rate hike. Asian equities edged higher after a volatile session for semiconductor stocks.

title: SK Hynix Shares Rebound After Early Rout as AI Jitters Persist
summary: SK Hynix Inc. erased an early plunge to climb more than 2% in Seoul on Tuesday, underscoring the extreme volatility gripping one of the world’s biggest beneficiaries of the artificial intelligence boom.

title: What to Expect From Wall Street Banks Earnings
summary: Analysts expect JPMorgan Chase & Co., Bank of America, Citigroup, Goldman Sachs Group and Morgan Stanley to report nearly $39 billion in trading revenue for the second quarter. Bloomberg's Balázs Penz breaks down the situation. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: UK’s Miliband Seen as Least Appealing Chancellor: Markets Pulse
summary: Andy Burnham has yet to announce his choice for UK Chancellor of the Exchequer, but among the presumptive next prime minister’s leading candidates, Energy Secretary Ed Miliband is seen as the least market-friendly choice, according to the latest Bloomberg Markets Live Pulse survey.

title: China’s Green-Tech Exports Surge on Energy Transition Demand
summary: China’s exports of green-tech products rose by more than a third in the first half of the year, driven by the accelerating global energy transition.

title: China Expels Former Politburo Member Over Graft and Sex Charges
summary: China expelled former Politburo member Ma Xingrui from the ruling Communist Party, accusing him of a wide range of offenses including corruption, abuse of power and trading political favors for sex.

title: South Korean Won Climbs to Two-Month High on Hynix ADR Inflows
summary: The South Korean won advanced to its strongest level since May on expectations that dollar inflows from SK Hynix Inc’s American depositary receipts will enter the local currency market.

title: Japan Bond Sale Draws High Demand After Katayama’s GPIF Remarks
summary: Japan’s 20-year government bond auction Tuesday attracted strong investor demand after remarks by Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama boosted sentiment toward sovereign debt.

title: Gold Holds Drop as Mideast Risks, Waller Remarks Boost Headwinds
summary: Gold steadied after a two-day decline, as escalating hostilities in the Middle East drove a surge in energy costs and a US monetary policymaker raised the likelihood of interest-rate hikes to rein in stubborn inflation.

title: Palm Oil Advances as Crude Rally Boosts Outlook for Biofuels
summary: Palm oil climbed for a second day as a surge in crude prices fueled by renewed US-Iran hostilities brightened demand prospects for biofuels.

title: South Korea Plans FX Rule Easing to Expand Won’s Global Use
summary: South Korea plans to relax foreign-exchange rules governing capital transactions in the second half of the year as part of a broader effort to internationalize the won and deepen overseas use of the currency.

title: India Asks State Banks to Step Up Foreign Currency Deposits
summary: India has asked state-owned banks to intensify efforts to garner foreign currency deposits from the 35-million-strong diaspora, as the country seeks to boost its external buffers and shore up the flagging rupee.

title: Australia Sees No Mass Layoffs From AI Just Yet
summary: A first-of-its-kind report by the Australian government finds AI has not yet caused broad disruption in the nation’s labor market. That’s as the rising demand for AI is heightening concerns over the future of white-collar jobs. Scientia Professor of AI at the University of New South Wales Toby Walsh joins "Bloomberg: The Asia Trade" to discuss why Canberra needs to act now to mitigate the impact of AI and prepare workers for change. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Oil Rises as Trump Resumes Hormuz Blockade | The Asia Trade 7/14/2026
summary: "Bloomberg: The Asia Trade" brings you everything you need to know to get ahead as the trading day begins in Asia. Bloomberg TV is live from Tokyo and Singapore with Shery Ahn and Avril Hong, getting insight and analysis from newsmakers and industry leaders on the biggest stories shaping global markets. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Oil Tops $85 a Barrel as Trump Reinstates Naval Blockade on Iran
summary: Brent oil rose above $85 a barrel for the first time in a month, as US President Donald Trump reimposed a blockade on Iranian ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz and demanded payment for all other cargo.

title: US, Iran Check the Oil Price Before Pushing Limits
summary: Trump’s 20% levy is the latest example of seeing what they can get away with.

title: UNSW's Parker on US' 20% Cargo Toll in Strait of Hormuz
summary: Jennifer Parker, Adjunct Fellow at UNSW Canberra, discusses the legality and diplomatic implications of the US blockade and 20 percent cargo toll in the Strait of Hormuz. She draws on more than 20 years' experience in the Royal Australian Navy, including leading multinational maritime operations in the Middle East. Jennifer speaks with Haidi Stroud-Watts on "Insight with Haslinda Amin." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: China’s Crude Imports Plunge to Lowest in Nearly a Decade
summary: Chinese crude oil imports fell sharply in June to near a decade low, hampered by war in the Persian Gulf and an abrupt slowdown in domestic demand.

title: China Trade Numbers Top All Forecasts in June 2026
summary: Here's a quick look at the numbers as China's exports surge in June while imports rise at the fastest clip in five years.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Iran Sneaking Out Tankers Via Hormuz as Trump Amps Up Threats
summary: Iran has been sneaking oil tankers through the Strait of Hormuz over the past few days, even before the US said it would reimpose a blockade of Iranian ports as hostilities escalated.

title: Phillippine Peso Approaches Record Low as Oil Prices Advance
summary: The Philippine peso falls toward a record low as escalating tensions in the Middle East drove oil prices higher, putting renewed pressure on the currency.

title: Leveraged Chip Bets Backfire in Korea as Biggest ETF Falls 45%
summary: A new breed of leveraged products tracking major South Korean chip stocks are tumbling in value, threatening steep losses for the country’s retail investors who favor the tools to pursue amplified returns.

title: Yuan Still Cheap Versus Euro Even After Gain, Deutsche Bank Says
summary: China’s yuan is still undervalued versus the euro despite its recent gains, and that’s contributing to a wider European Union trade deficit, according to Deutsche Bank AG.

title: Taiwan Fund Profits 80% After Intervening to Blunt Trump Tariffs
summary: Taiwan reported a roughly 80% profit from a nine-month-long intervention to support the island’s stock market following the Trump administration’s move to impose tariffs, though it has now exited its position.

title: US Hits Iran With Strikes, Blockade as Trump Plans Hormuz Charge
summary: President Donald Trump reinstated the US blockade of Iranian ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz and demanded a 20% reimbursement for all other cargo shipped through the waterway.
